rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Defendants Motion To Enforce Stay Pending Appeals
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 27, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT ANNE KIHAGI, JULIA MWANGI, XELAN PROP 1, LLC, RENKA PROP, LLC, NOZARI 2, LLC, ZORIALL, LLC, CHRISTINE MWANGI Amended Notice Of Defendants Motion To Enforce Stay Pending Appeals is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1013. Amended Notice of Motion filed on April 6, 2021 for a hearing on April 27, 2021. To the extent April 27, 2021 was intended to be a continued hearing date from April 14, 2021, no leave of court was requested or granted for such continuance; April 14, 2021 hearing was effectively taken off calendar by filing of the Amended Notice. The Court also notes that no courtesy copies of the filings in support of this motion with April 27, 2021 hearing date have been provided to the Court. SFLR 2.7B. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 3401 CLAY STREET JOINT VENTURE, VS. TIMOTHY BAKA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike The Eighth Cause Of Action For Libel In The Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 27, 2021 line 4. PLAINTIFF 3401 CLAY STREET JOINT VENTURE, Notice Of Motion And Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike The Eighth Cause Of Action For Libel In The Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. Moving party has shifted its burden under the first prong and opposition failed to establish probability of prevailing on the merits. See Abraham v. Lancaster Community Hospital (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 796. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: DELAWARE LAKEWOOD APARTMENT, LLC VS. LILIYA GRABOVETSKAYA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 27, 2021 line 6. DEFENDANT LILIYA GRABOVETSKAYA MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The operative notice provides sufficient grounds to support a UD action. Moving party cites no authority for the proposition that parts of exhibits to a complaint are subject to motion to strike, including typos. Furthermore, no authority for the proposition that suggested method of cure of one of the asserted violations renders the entire notice defective is not supported by any applicable authority. Cases regarding non-payment of rent are inapposite as they involve one asserted violation (failure to pay rent) and one improper demand for cure (overstated rent). Meet and confer requirements are not applicable. CCP 435.5(d)(2). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
